sides.position: The presence of the universal Sovereign leading his people - the ark not of a local deity but of the Lord of the whole world, sovereign over nature and the nations, who commands the river and drives out the Canaanites, the whole earth the LORD's (Psalm 24:1; Zechariah 4:14), a figure of Christ the Lord of all leading his people through every flood

schools.summary: That the priests stood firm and all Israel passed over on dry ground: 'and the priests that bare the ark of the covenant of the LORD stood firm on dry ground in the midst of Jordan, and all the Israelites passed over on dry ground, until all the people were passed clean over Jordan' — the dry-ground passage — 'and the priests that bare the ark of the covenant of the LORD stood firm on dry ground in the midst of Jordan, and all the Israelites passed over on dry ground, until all the people were passed clean over Jordan': the priests stood firm with the ark in the dry riverbed while the whole nation crossed over on dry ground (the witnesses noting the significance — the complete and glorious deliverance of all Israel through the parted waters, the priests bearing the ark standing firm while the people passed, the river that was their barrier made their highway, the whole nation passing safely on dry ground, the lesson — that God opens a way for his people through every barrier, the waters that should have drowned them made dry land beneath their feet, the deliverance complete, 'all the people were passed clean over,' none lost, the truth that this crossing answered to the passage of the Red Sea, the same God dividing the waters, 'thou leddest thy people... through the deep, as an horse in the wilderness,' Isaiah 63:13; Psalm 66:6, the figure — the passage through the waters a figure of salvation, of baptism, and of the believer's passage through death into the inheritance, 'they were all baptized... in the sea,' 1 Corinthians 10:1-2, the truth that Christ, the Ark, stands firm in the midst while his people pass safely through, the figure of the way opened through death into the promised rest, the Jordan a type of the believer's last passage, the priests standing firm the pledge of the presence that holds back the flood, the certainty that God brings all his people clean over, the figure of the great deliverance through the waters, all Israel passing over on dry ground); debates.question: What does the passage over Jordan on dry ground signify?

sides.position: The great deliverance of God's people through the waters - the priests standing firm with the ark while the whole nation passed safely on dry ground, none lost (Psalm 66:6), answering to the passage of the Red Sea (Isaiah 63:13), a figure of salvation and baptism (1 Corinthians 10:1-2), Christ the Ark standing firm in the midst while his people pass through into the inheritance

themes: And the priests that bare the ark of the covenant of the LORD stood firm on dry ground in the midst of Jordan, and all the Israelites passed over on dry ground, until all the people were passed clean over Jordan
that the priests stood firm and all Israel passed over on dry ground (the crux of the great deliverance through the waters, a figure of salvation)
the complete deliverance of all Israel through the parted waters, the river made their highway and the whole nation passing safely, none lost
answering to the Red Sea, a figure of salvation and baptism, Christ the Ark standing firm while his people pass through (Psalm 66:6; Isaiah 63:13; 1 Corinthians 10:1-2)
